module_info.docstring:
Control Flow Graph (CFG) extraction for multi-language code analysis.

Provides CFG extraction for:
- Python (via ast module)
- TypeScript/JavaScript (via tree-sitter)
- Go (via tree-sitter)
- Rust (via tree-sitter)

Based on staticfg pattern but simplified for TLDR-code use case.

classes.name: CFGBlock
classes.docstring:
Basic block - sequential statements with no internal branches.

A basic block is a sequence of statements where:
- Control enters only at the first statement
- Control leaves only at the last statement

methods.name: get_source
methods.docstring:
Get source code for this block from the original code.

Args:
    source_code: The full source code of the file/function.

Returns:
    The source code lines for this block.

classes.name: CFGEdge
classes.docstring:
Edge between blocks with optional branch condition.

Edge types:
- "true" / "false": conditional branch outcomes
- "unconditional": direct flow (sequential, function call)
- "back_edge": loop iteration (body -> guard)
- "break": exit loop early
- "continue": skip to next iteration
- "iterate": for-loop enters body
- "exhausted": for-loop exits when iterator is done

classes.name: CFGInfo
classes.docstring:
Control flow graph for a function.

Provides:
- Basic block structure
- Control flow edges with conditions
- Entry/exit points
- Cyclomatic complexity metric
- Nested function CFGs (closures, inner functions)

classes.name: PythonCFGBuilder
classes.docstring:
Build CFG from Python AST.

Based on staticfg pattern but simplified for our needs.
We track:
- Basic blocks with line numbers
- Edges with types and conditions
- Loop guards for back edges
- Decision points for cyclomatic complexity

methods.name: new_block
methods.docstring: Create a new block and add it to the graph.

methods.name: add_edge
methods.docstring: Add an edge between blocks.

methods.name: build
methods.docstring: Build CFG from a function definition node.

methods.name: visit_FunctionDef
methods.docstring: Handle nested function definitions - build sub-CFG.

methods.name: visit_AsyncFunctionDef
methods.docstring: Handle nested async function definitions - build sub-CFG.

methods.name: _get_condition_str
methods.docstring: Convert AST condition node to readable string.

methods.name: _extract_calls_shallow
methods.docstring:
Extract function call names from a single statement (not nested blocks).

For compound statements (if, for, while), only extracts from the condition,
not from the body. Body calls are extracted when those blocks are visited.

methods.name: _get_call_name
methods.docstring: Extract the function name from a Call node.

methods.name: _add_calls_to_block
methods.docstring: Extract and add function calls from statement to block.

methods.name: visit_If
methods.docstring: Handle if/elif/else statements - creates diamond pattern.

methods.name: visit_While
methods.docstring: Handle while loops - creates loop with back edge.

methods.name: visit_For
methods.docstring: Handle for loops - similar to while but with iterator.

methods.name: visit_Return
methods.docstring: Handle return statements - marks exit block.

methods.name: visit_Break
methods.docstring: Handle break - edge to after-loop block.

methods.name: visit_Continue
methods.docstring: Handle continue - edge to loop guard.

methods.name: generic_visit
methods.docstring: Visit children for compound statements we don't handle specially.

classes.name: TreeSitterCFGBuilder
classes.docstring:
Build CFG from tree-sitter parse tree.

Works for TypeScript, JavaScript, Go, Rust, Ruby, and Swift.

methods.name: get_node_text
methods.docstring: Get source text for a node.

methods.name: build
methods.docstring: Build CFG from a function node.

methods.name: _find_function_body
methods.docstring: Find the body/block child of a function node.

methods.name: _visit_node
methods.docstring: Visit a tree-sitter node and build CFG.

methods.name: _visit_ruby_call
methods.docstring: Handle Ruby method calls, especially iterators like .each.

methods.name: _find_child_by_type
methods.docstring: Find first child matching any of the given types.

methods.name: _find_child_by_field
methods.docstring: Find child by field name.

methods.name: _visit_if
methods.docstring: Handle if/else statements.

methods.name: _visit_while
methods.docstring: Handle while loops.

methods.name: _visit_for
methods.docstring: Handle for loops.

methods.name: _visit_loop
methods.docstring: Handle Rust's infinite loop (loop {}).

methods.name: _visit_repeat
methods.docstring:
Handle Lua's repeat-until loops.

repeat-until is unique: body executes at least once, then condition is checked.
Unlike while (condition checked first), the body always runs first.

methods.name: _visit_return
methods.docstring: Handle return statements.

methods.name: _visit_break
methods.docstring: Handle break statements.

methods.name: _visit_continue
methods.docstring: Handle continue statements.

methods.name: _visit_guard
methods.docstring: Handle Swift guard statements - guard let/guard else pattern.

methods.name: _visit_switch
methods.docstring: Handle switch/case statements.

classes.name: ElixirCFGBuilder
classes.docstring:
Build CFG from Elixir tree-sitter parse tree.

Elixir has a unique AST where everything is a macro/call:
- def/defp are function definitions (macros)
- if/case/cond/with are control flow (macros)
- Function body is in do_block

methods.name: get_node_text
methods.docstring: Get source text for a node.

methods.name: _get_call_name
methods.docstring: Get the name of a call node (e.g., 'if', 'def', 'case').

methods.name: build
methods.docstring: Build CFG from an Elixir function node.

methods.name: _visit_node
methods.docstring: Visit a tree-sitter node and build CFG.

methods.name: _visit_if
methods.docstring: Handle Elixir if expressions.

methods.name: _visit_case
methods.docstring: Handle Elixir case expressions.

methods.name: _visit_cond
methods.docstring: Handle Elixir cond expressions.

methods.name: _visit_with
methods.docstring: Handle Elixir with expressions.

functions.name: extract_python_cfg
functions.docstring:
Extract CFG for a specific function from Python source code.

Args:
    source: Python source code as string
    function_name: Name of the function to extract CFG for

Returns:
    CFGInfo dataclass with blocks, edges, and complexity

Raises:
    ValueError: If function not found in source

functions.name: _get_ts_parser
functions.docstring: Get or create a tree-sitter parser for the given language.

functions.name: _find_function_node
functions.docstring: Find function node in tree-sitter tree by name.

functions.docstring: Extract CFG for a TypeScript/JavaScript function.

functions.docstring: Extract CFG for a Go function.

functions.docstring: Extract CFG for a Rust function.

functions.name: extract_c_cfg
functions.docstring: Extract CFG for a C function.

functions.name: extract_cpp_cfg
functions.docstring: Extract CFG for a C++ function.

functions.name: extract_php_cfg
functions.docstring:
Extract CFG for a PHP function.

Args:
    source: PHP source code (may include <?php tag)
    function_name: Name of function to extract CFG for

Returns:
    CFGInfo with blocks, edges, and complexity

Raises:
    ImportError: If tree-sitter-php is not available
    ValueError: If function not found in source

functions.docstring: Extract CFG for a Ruby method.

functions.name: _find_lua_function_by_name
functions.docstring:
Find a Lua function node by name in tree-sitter tree.

Handles both:
- function name() ... end (function_declaration)
- local function name() ... end (function_declaration with local)
- function Table.name() ... end (table method)
- function Table:name() ... end (table method with self)

functions.name: _find_elixir_function_node
functions.docstring:
Find Elixir function node by name.

In Elixir, functions are defined with `def` or `defp` macros:
- def function_name(args) do ... end
- defp private_function(args) do ... end

The tree-sitter AST represents these as:
- call node with identifier "def" or "defp"
- arguments contain another call node with the function name

functions.name: extract_elixir_cfg
functions.docstring:
Extract CFG for an Elixir function.

Args:
    source: Elixir source code
    function_name: Name of function to extract CFG for

Returns:
    CFGInfo with blocks, edges, and complexity

Raises:
    ImportError: If tree-sitter-elixir is not available
    ValueError: If function not found in source
